svg.style: max-width: 100%; height: auto; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
svg.title: License tiers — protocol open, implementation source-available, state private
svg.desc: Motebit's license model has three tiers. The protocol floor (specs, types, crypto, SDK) is Apache-2.0 with an explicit patent grant — anyone can implement it, in any language, without permission. The reference implementation (runtime, engines, services, apps) is BSL 1.1, source-available today and converting to Apache-2.0 four years after each version's release. The accumulated state a relay generates by running — trust graph, federation routes, signed audit ledger — belongs to whoever runs the relay; it is never licensed, mirrored, or shared. Both license families converge to a single Apache-2.0 posture at the Change Date.

g.text: specs, types, crypto suite, SDK, hardware-attestation verifiers · permanent · patent grant
runtime, engines, services, apps · source-available now · → Apache-2.0 four years after release
trust graph, federation routes, signed audit ledger · belongs to whoever runs the relay · never licensed
Open the protocol so anyone can interoperate. Source-available the implementation so anyone can run it. Keep the state.
